name: Recipe 2 — Timeout: what happens when no one resumes (synthetic)
comment: The yielded callback fires even in the absence of a resume, after NEP-519's fixed 200-block budget. The synthetic scene compresses the wait visually by using the block HUD's advancing counter across a long idle. At ~200 blocks past yield, the callback fires with PromiseError; the on_timeout_resumed handler matches on Err and emits recipe_resolved_err. Satellite settles with status="timeout" (red shockwave + ember).

events.title: Recipe 2 — timeout
events.body: what happens when no one resumes? NEP-519 guarantees the callback still fires — but with PromiseError in place of the resume payload. The 200-block budget is fixed.

events.title: tx1 — recipe_timeout_yield(name)
events.body: same shape as basic: Promise::new_yield, store YieldId. But there is no recipe_timeout_resume method — nothing will ever resume this yield.

events.title: waiting — no resume arrives
events.body: blocks pass. the yielded callback is still registered, prepaid gas is still locked. NEP-519's 200-block timeout is ticking.

events.title: budget exhausted
events.body: at yield + 200 blocks, the runtime delivers PromiseError to the callback receipt already waiting in the yield tx's DAG. It executes with #[callback_result] = Err(PromiseError).

events.title: resolved_err
events.body: on_timeout_resumed's Err arm ran; trace event recipe_resolved_err emitted. The callback fired exactly once, as promised.
